Input functionality for the debug serial port, including initialization and DMA-based receiving control.

# Purpose
This header file, `dbgserial_input.h`, defines the input-related functionality for a debug serial port driver. It includes function declarations for initializing the input components of the debug serial driver with [`dbgserial_input_init`](<#dbgserial_input_init>), registering a character callback function with [`dbgserial_register_character_callback`](<#dbgserial_register_character_callback>), and enabling external interrupt-based receiving with [`dbgserial_enable_rx_exti`](<#dbgserial_enable_rx_exti>). Additionally, it provides a function, [`dbgserial_set_rx_dma_enabled`](<#dbgserial_set_rx_dma_enabled>), to enable or disable DMA-based receiving. The file also defines a callback type, `DbgSerialCharacterCallback`, which allows handling of received characters and indicates if a context switch is necessary.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`
- `stdint.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### dbgserial\_input\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:dbgserial_input_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/dbgserial_input.h#L27>)

Initializes the input components of the debug serial driver.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up the necessary configurations for receiving data through the debug serial interface. It configures external interrupt lines and USART interrupt handlers to enable data reception. This function must be called before any data reception operations are performed to ensure that the input components are correctly initialized and ready to handle incoming data.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`dbgserial_input_init`](<dbgserial_input.c.md#dbgserial_input_init>)  (Implementation)


---
### dbgserial\_register\_character\_callback<!-- {{#callable_declaration:dbgserial_register_character_callback}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/dbgserial_input.h#L30>)

Registers a callback for character reception events.
- **Description**: Use this function to set a callback that the system will call when a character is received through the debug serial port. This function must be called after initializing the debug serial input with `dbgserial_input_init`. The callback can be used to process incoming characters and decide if a context switch is necessary. Ensure that the callback function is valid and correctly handles the character input.
- **Inputs**:
    - `callback`: A function pointer to a `DbgSerialCharacterCallback` that takes a character and a pointer to a boolean. The callback must not be null. The system will call this function when a character is received. The boolean pointer allows the callback to indicate if a context switch should occur.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`dbgserial_register_character_callback`](<dbgserial_input.c.md#dbgserial_register_character_callback>)  (Implementation)


---
### dbgserial\_enable\_rx\_exti<!-- {{#callable_declaration:dbgserial_enable_rx_exti}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/dbgserial_input.h#L32>)

Enables the external interrupt for receiving data on the debug serial port.
- **Description**: Use this function to activate the external interrupt for the debug serial port's receive functionality. This function should be called when you want the system to start listening for incoming data on the debug serial port. Ensure that the debug serial port is properly initialized before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`dbgserial_enable_rx_exti`](<dbgserial_input.c.md#dbgserial_enable_rx_exti>)  (Implementation)


---
### dbgserial\_set\_rx\_dma\_enabled<!-- {{#callable_declaration:dbgserial_set_rx_dma_enabled}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/console/dbgserial_input.h#L35>)

Enables or disables DMA-based receiving for the debug serial port.
- **Description**: Use this function to control whether the debug serial port uses DMA for receiving data. This function must be called after the debug serial port is initialized. It is important to note that on certain targets, such as QEMU, DMA cannot be used, and the function will automatically disable DMA in such cases. The function has no effect if the DMA state is already set to the desired value.
- **Inputs**:
    - `enabled`: A boolean value indicating whether to enable (true) or disable (false) DMA-based receiving. The caller retains ownership of this parameter.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`dbgserial_set_rx_dma_enabled`](<dbgserial_input.c.md#dbgserial_set_rx_dma_enabled>)  (Implementation)
